analysis.raw_memory_comparison: Compare raw_memory directly with validated_lessons as a secondary analysis of error propagation; it does not determine primary success.

change_control.after_pilot: Do not change labels, prompts, success thresholds, memory policy, dataset, or evaluator policy after viewing held-out results. Record unavoidable changes as protocol deviations and run them under a new contract version.
change_control.before_pilot: Changes are allowed but must increment contract_version.

dataset.evidence_snapshot_policy: Retrieve and cache evidence before comparative runs. All conditions must use identical cached evidence. Record retrieval timestamps and query parameters.
dataset.leakage_policy: Gold labels, gold rationales, and held-out or distribution-shift evaluator feedback must never be supplied to the assessor, lesson generator, or memory.

experiment.actual_run_timestamp_policy: Record an ISO 8601 UTC timestamp in every run artifact.
experiment.framing: research evidence triage, not medical advice or a treatment recommendation
experiment.name: self-improving-biomedical-evidence-triage
experiment.primary_question: Does evaluator-validated procedural lesson memory improve held-out evidence triage without increasing unsupported claims or error propagation?

failure_and_inconclusive_rules.failure: The experiment fails if validated_lessons increases held-out unsupported-claim rate, causes any held-out error attributable to an incorrect validated memory, or trails no_memory on held-out macro-F1.
failure_and_inconclusive_rules.inconclusive: The result is inconclusive when it is neither a success nor a failure, including a positive held-out macro-F1 change smaller than 0.05 with no safety regression.

success_criterion.decision_rule: The experiment succeeds only if validated_lessons improves held-out macro-F1 by at least 0.05 absolute, does not increase held-out unsupported-claim rate, and causes zero held-out errors attributable to an incorrect validated memory.
